name: Trail of the Necropolises
name_original: Necrópolis de Cuyacabras y Eremitorios de Quintanar de la Sierra — circular por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda
description: Hike a moderate 10.6-mile loop through Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda, exploring ancient rock-cut tombs and hermitages.
region_intro: Hiking around Quintanar De La Sierra offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de la Demanda and the Parque Natural de las Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes, extensive pine forests, and lacustrine areas. Notable features include impressive rock formations, river canyons, and high-altitude lagoons. The area provides a range of trails suitable for various experience levels. Best hiking trails around Quintanar De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Lagunas de Neila Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic lacustrine areas of the Sierra de la Demanda. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Lagunas de Neila — circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain within the natural space, including views of the lagoons. Local hikers also love the Trail of the Necropolises, a 10.6 miles (17.0 km) trail leading through pine and oak forests to an archaeological site, often completed in about 4 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Quintanar De La Sierra is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive pine forests, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Quintanar De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Quintanar De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Quintanar De La Sierra?
A: Quintanar De La Sierra offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 50 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to various preferences, from leisurely strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Quintanar De La Sierra?
A: The region is characterized by diverse terrain, including mountainous landscapes, extensive pine forests (especially Scots pine), and unique lacustrine areas. You'll encounter impressive rock formations, river canyons, and high-altitude lagoons, particularly in areas like the Sierra de la Demanda and the Parque Natural de las Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Quintanar De La Sierra?
A: Yes, Quintanar De La Sierra offers several easy trails suitable for families and beginners. For instance, the region research mentions a 'Family Route' around a nearby reservoir. Overall, 11 of the available routes are classified as easy, providing gentle paths to enjoy the natural beauty.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover several impressive natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt; is a beautiful glacial lagoon. You might also encounter the picturesque Cascada de El Chorlón waterfall and the unique karstic landscape of Las Calderas, which features a series of captivating waterfalls, caves, and lakes. For panoramic views, consider the path leading to the Ermita Virgen del Monte.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, for those interested in history, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041378881/trail-of-the-necropolises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Necropolises&lt;/a&gt; leads to the Necrópolis de Cuyacabras. This archaeological site, surrounded by pines and oaks, features 166 anthropomorphic tombs and 13 niches carved into the rock, dating back to the Early Middle Ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Quintanar De La Sierra?
A: Quintanar De La Sierra is suitable for hiking across various seasons. The region's diverse landscapes, from forests to high-altitude areas, offer different experiences. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is ideal for exploring higher elevations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;. Winter hikes can be beautiful, especially in the pine forests, but may require appropriate gear for snow and ice.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, the region offers circular routes. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041405818/las-lagunas-de-neila-circular-by-the-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lagunas de Neila — circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;, which provides varied terrain and views of the lagoons. The Ruta Circular por las Peñas (PR-CV 21) also winds through lush forests and striking rocky areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Quintanar De La Sierra?
A: The hiking routes in Quintanar De La Sierra are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from extensive pine forests to unique rock formations, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, there are 14 difficult routes available. The region's mountainous terrain, including ascents to peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt; (2,228 meters), offers demanding trails with significant elevation changes and rewarding views. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19882976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Laguna (2004m) – Campiña loop from Puerto El Collado&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 17.8 km route.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the hiking trails?
A: Quintanar De La Sierra is a charming village that offers amenities for visitors. While specific cafes or accommodations directly on trails are not detailed, the village itself provides options for dining and rural accommodation, making it a convenient base for exploring the surrounding hiking areas.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is home to several beautiful waterfalls. You can discover the picturesque Cascada de El Chorlón, and the unique karstic landscape of Las Calderas features a series of captivating waterfalls. Additionally, hikers ascending towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt; may encounter the scenic waterfall of the Arroyo de La Laguna.

Q: What is the highest point I can reach on a hike in the area?
A: The highest point you can reach in the immediate area is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, standing at 2,228 meters in the Sierra de Urbión. This peak offers superb photographic opportunities and is also close to the source of the Duero River.

Medieval funerary city in the province of Burgos bordering Soria, making it very accessible from both provinces. It is located after the wooden house, continuing along the path. In this necropolis you can perfectly observe the funerary niches that have been created in the stones over the years.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is rated as moderate. The terrain primarily consists of paths through dense pine and oak forests, with some sections on rocky elevations, especially around the necropolis sites. You'll experience gentle slopes at lower elevations and some craggy rock formations. The total elevation gain is around 290 meters over approximately 17 kilometers.

faqs.question: What historical and natural landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in historical sites. You will encounter the extensive Early Medieval Necropolis of Cuyacabras, featuring numerous rock-cut tombs. Further along, you'll find the Cueva de Andrés Hermitage, a beautiful example of a Mozarabic cave-hermitage, and the Hermitage of la Cerca. Another significant stop is the Necropolis of Comunero de Revenga. The natural scenery includes extensive pine and oak forests, and sometimes beech trees, within the mountainous landscape of the Sierra de la Demanda.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the entire circular route are not provided, the Necropolis of Cuyacabras is accessible by car, with only a short walk (around five minutes) required to reach the site from a well-marked path. It is advisable to look for designated parking areas near Quintanar de la Sierra or at the main access points to the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this trail within the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda. However, it's always good practice to check local regulations before your visit, especially for protected natural areas. Always respect the natural environment and any signage you encounter.

faqs.question: Is this circular trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and the accessibility of some key historical sites, parts of this route, particularly those leading directly to the Necropolis of Cuyacabras, are suitable for families and those seeking a leisurely exploration. The full 17 km circular route might be a bit long for very young children or absolute beginners, but it is generally manageable for those with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during spring, summer, and autumn. Spring offers lush greenery and abundant water in rivers, while autumn provides beautiful fall colors. Summer is also suitable, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter hiking is possible, but some higher elevation areas in the Sierra de la Demanda can experience snow, so check weather conditions beforehand.

faqs.question: Is the Necropolis of Cuyacabras and Eremitorios circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in natural parks like the Sierra de la Demanda, often on a leash. Given the presence of historical sites and potential wildlife, it is highly recommended to keep your dog on a leash at all times and to clean up after them. Always respect local signage regarding pets.

faqs.question: Does this circular route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Ruta del Sureste, Camino de las Tabladas, Camino la Horca, Camino San Martín, and Puente de Cuevalarza, among others. This offers opportunities to explore different parts of the region if you wish to extend your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail, like cafes or restrooms?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is primarily within a natural area, so direct facilities like restrooms or cafes on the route are unlikely. However, the nearby village of Quintanar de la Sierra offers amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and possibly accommodation options where you can find refreshments and services before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What makes this trail particularly unique or special?
faqs.answer: This trail is exceptional for its unparalleled blend of natural beauty and profound historical significance. It features one of Europe's largest concentrations of Paleochristian rock-cut tombs at the Early Medieval Necropolis of Cuyacabras, dating back to the 10th century. The presence of the Mozarabic Cueva de Andrés Hermitage further highlights its archaeological importance, offering a rare glimpse into medieval monastic life within a stunning forest setting.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point of the trail?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the specific trailhead might be limited as it's located within a natural area. It's best to check bus schedules to Quintanar de la Sierra, the nearest significant village. From there, you might need to arrange local transport or walk to the starting point of the circular route.
